title: The 5 types of real world assets being tokenized fastest onchain
summary: Tokenized Real World Assets span treasuries, real estate, stocks, commodities and private credit. The sector is still small in TradFi terms but it's growing very, very fast.
body:
Tokenization, the process of converting real-world assets into digital tokens on a blockchain, is gaining momentum. This innovation is making traditionally illiquid assets like real estate and private credit more accessible to a broader range of investors. The sector is still small compared to traditional finance, but it's growing at an unprecedented pace.

According to the source, the fastest-growing categories of tokenized assets include treasuries, real estate, stocks, commodities, and private credit. Each of these categories offers unique benefits, such as fractional ownership and increased liquidity. For example, tokenized real estate allows investors to buy shares of properties without needing to purchase an entire building, making it easier to diversify their portfolios.

This trend matters to everyday people because it democratizes investment opportunities. Tokenization lowers the barriers to entry, allowing smaller investors to access assets that were previously only available to institutional players. For instance, tokenized stocks enable fractional ownership, meaning investors can buy a portion of a share rather than the whole thing, making high-value stocks more attainable.

As the tokenization sector continues to evolve, investors should watch for regulatory developments and new platforms offering tokenized assets. Keeping an eye on these trends can help individuals make informed decisions about diversifying their portfolios with tokenized real-world assets.
